task.title: Incorporate MCP Server into Golem CLI

data.title: Incorporate MCP Server into Golem CLI
data.body:
With a new command, Golem CLI enters serve mode:

```bash
%> golem-cli --serve --serve-port 1232
%> golem-cli running MCP Server at port 1232
```

In this mode, Golem CLI creates an MCP Server that exposes different commands as different tools, and exposes relevant resources (the manifest file in the current, ancestor, and children directories) as resources.

When this ticket is completed, it should be possible to use an agent such as Claude Code to perform anything that Golem CLI can do. Moreover, all individual tools and resources must be end-to-end tested with an MCP Client that interacts with the MCP Server.

[This library](https://github.com/rust-mcp-stack/rust-mcp-sdk) looks like the one to use for Rust, but perhaps there are better options available.

task.title: [Enhancement]: WordPress + OpenLiteSpeed service template

data.title: [Enhancement]: WordPress + OpenLiteSpeed service template
data.body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

Following up on the discussion in #8255 (which was closed to split the scope into two dedicated issues).
As a web agency (Mediastar Web), we manage multiple WordPress sites for clients and we're evaluating Coolify as our central hosting platform. One area where we're missing structured support is a production-ready reference setup for running WordPress on OpenLiteSpeed.
What we'd like to see:
- A reusable service template or stack for WordPress + OpenLiteSpeed, following the existing Coolify template patterns
- Persistent volumes for WordPress files and database data
- Compatibility with Coolify's built-in proxy

- Compatibility with Coolify's built-in proxy

task.title: [Enhancement]: Add a reusable email server stack/template for managing client email domains

data.title: [Enhancement]: Add a reusable email server stack/template for managing client email domains
data.body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

Following up on the discussion in #8255 (which was closed to split the scope into two dedicated issues).
As a web agency (Mediastar Web), we manage email services for multiple client domains and we're evaluating Coolify as our central hosting platform. One area where we're missing structured support is a straightforward way to host and manage client email domains through a reusable template or stack.
What we'd like to see:
- A reusable service template/stack for a self-hosted email server (e.g., based on Docker Mailserver, Mailu, or a similar well-maintained project)
- Persistent volumes for mailboxes, configuration, and certificates
- Compatibility with Coolify's proxy and certificate management

task.title: Replace panic with error handling in template loader when dialers are missing

data.title: Replace panic with error handling in template loader when dialers are missing
data.body:
## Description

The template loader in `pkg/catalog/loader/loader.go` (around lines 684-703) currently panics when `protocolstate.GetDialersWithId()` returns nil. This is too harsh for a public API that may be exercised in non-scanning flows.

## Current Behavior

When dialers are not initialized, the code panics:
```go
dialers := protocolstate.GetDialersWithId(typesOpts.ExecutionId)
if dialers == nil {
    panic("dialers with executionId " + typesOpts.ExecutionId + " not found")
}
```

## Expected Behavior

The loader should return a proper error instead of panicking, allowing callers to handle the situation gracefully. The broader codebase pattern returns `fmt.Errorf` when dialers are missing rather than panicking.

## Suggested Changes

1. Replace the panic with proper error return (e.g., `return fmt.Errorf("dialers with executionId %s not found", typesOpts.ExecutionId)`)
2. Update the loader function signature to propagate the error
3. Update callers to handle the error appropriately
4. Add documentation noting the new error condition

## Context

Callers like lazy auth template loading (`internal/runner/lazy.go`) and automatic tech detection (`pkg/protocols/common/automaticscan/`) invoke `LoadTemplates`/`LoadTemplatesWithTags` without guaranteed dialer initialization.

## References

- PR: https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/pull/6668
- Comment: https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/pull/6668#discussion_r2596513576
- Requested by: @dwisiswant0

## Context

## References

- PR: https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/pull/6668

- Requested by: @dwisiswant0
